raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A drawer is open, containing multiple bags of snacks. Visible brands include 'Dang', 'Sensat', and 'Savannah'. The bags are arranged in rows, with some partially overlapping. The drawer is metallic and has a handle on the right side. STRATEGY - The task is to manipulate the drawer's contents. A logical first step is to grasp the drawer handle to either pull it out further or push it in. This is a prerequisite for accessing or organizing the items inside. Failure mode: if the handle is not grasped, the drawer cannot be moved. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1: reach the drawer handle. The handle is a distinct part of the drawer, located on the right side. Grasping it will allow for linear motion (pull or push) along the drawer's axis. Step 2: grasp the handle. This provides a secure grip for the subsequent pull action. Step 3: pull the drawer. This action will extend the drawer, making the contents more accessible for further manipulation.
Goal: all_on(snack_bags, drawer); stable(snack_bags)
Scene: drawer, snack_bags
Step 1: reach(drawer)
  affordance_region: the handle of the drawer
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, drawer, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, drawer) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(drawer)
  affordance_region: the handle of the drawer
  contact: holding(drawer) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(drawer)
Step 3: pull(drawer)
  affordance_region: the handle of the drawer
  contact: holding(drawer) [progress]
  spatial: distance(drawer, drawer_frame, >, 0.1) [completion]
  direction: pull_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(drawer, drawer_frame) > 0.1
<END_OF_PLAN>
